game_name: Solar Jetman: Hunt for the Golden Warpship

danger_signals.meaning: inhabited hostile caverns; player must avoid or destroy to progress safely

danger_signals.visual_form: visual feedback through pod inertia behavior; changing environmental layout per planet
danger_signals.meaning: each planet presents different gravitational hazards; difficulty escalates with inertia effects

danger_signals.visual_form: pod explodes; Jetman ejects into space suit state
danger_signals.meaning: immediate danger; vulnerable state without pod protection; death if player cannot reach mothership

danger_signals.visual_form: fuel meter visual indicator (HUD); pod becomes non-functional
danger_signals.meaning: mission failure state; player cannot complete objectives or return to mothership without fuel

danger_signals.visual_form: deeper cavern environments; enemy density increases
danger_signals.meaning: environmental hazard escalation; wormholes and deeper sections are more dangerous

safety_signals.visual_form: immobile mothership sprite anchored at launch point
safety_signals.meaning: safe haven; location for pod recovery, fuel refueling, score cashing for power-ups
resource availability; fuel replenishment stations for mission continuation

safety_signals.visual_form: wormhole visual markers in cavern architecture
safety_signals.meaning: safe passage points; allow shortcutting through caverns and delivering items

safety_signals.visual_form: purchasable power-up menu after every other stage
safety_signals.meaning: player agency and progression; defensive/offensive capability enhancement

safety_signals.visual_form: Mapping Device item; reveals planet layout visually
safety_signals.meaning: navigation safety; reduces exploration risk through information provision

faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: pod appears as controllable unit; space suit as vulnerable alternate form
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: launches from mothership; collects items via tow cable

faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: enemy infested caverns; varied per planet ecology
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: distributed throughout cavern systems on each planet; density increases with depth
located at cavern endpoints; one piece per planet level

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: cavern environment with specific gravity visual signature; enemy placement visible
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: pod launches from mothership with specific inertia behavior; player must adapt to gravity

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: multiple fuel item sprites visible in cavern; HUD fuel meter shows high capacity
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: player can freely explore; extended flight duration possible

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: fuel meter visibly depleted; HUD warning indicator implied
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: pod performance degraded; player must navigate efficiently back to mothership

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: pod sprite disappears; Jetman space suit sprite appears in cavern
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: movement speed reduced; vulnerability increased; must reach mothership or die

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: item collected via tow cable; visual weight/drag on pod movement
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: pod maneuverability reduced; must deliver to mothership or wormhole deposit point

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: pod floats more freely; slower fall speed visual effect
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: inertia minimized; easier steering; early planet levels

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: pod falls rapidly; sharp inertia visual feedback
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: difficult maneuverability; late game planets present skill escalation challenge

environmental_storytelling.example: each planet's unique gravitational pull creates distinct environmental hazards and tells story of alien world physics; progression through planets with increasing gravity establishes world escalation
deeper caverns contain wormholes and Golden Warpship pieces; layout progression communicates that valuable artifacts are in dangerous depths
immobile mothership at start point establishes safe home base narrative; must return carrying items signals resource gathering quest structure
different enemy types inhabit different planets; enemy-infested caverns communicate that each world has native hostile fauna
fuel items distributed within caverns; fuel depletion as failure state communicates survival challenge and resource management narrative
Golden Warpship pieces placed across 12+ planets; sequential collection quest establishes grand journey narrative spanning multiple worlds

entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: responsive steering; subject to planet gravity; can fire weapons; tow cable operational
becomes non-functional; cannot navigate or return to mothership; mission failure
maneuverability reduced; drag increases; flight becomes difficult; visual weight indication
sprite disappears; eject animation; Jetman appears in space suit; player loses pod protection
agile movement possible; highly vulnerable to damage; must reach mothership or perish
inhabit caverns; threat to pod and spacewalking jetman; can be destroyed for points
stationary; receives items via tow cable delivery; refuels pod; resets Jetman when destroyed outside pod
slow descent; reduced inertia; easier navigation; less skill-dependent
rapid descent; strong inertia effects; difficult navigation; high skill requirement

notes: Solar Jetman uses gravity variance as primary world-state differentiator across planets. Danger communication relies on enemy placement density, fuel mechanics as existential threat, and gravity difficulty curves. The mothership functions as visual safety anchor. Pod destruction state transition to space suit represents critical vulnerability narrative moment. Item collection with drag penalty creates mechanical tension that communicates environmental hazard. Limited source material on specific visual design; extraction based on gameplay systems and their visual manifestations documented in Wikipedia and TCRF sources.
